title: ‘Nobody wants to see this’: Knicks fan Rich Eisen’s rant about the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Rich Eisen expresses frustration over the Celtics' unexpected success amid roster changes and Jayson Tatum's injury.
Eisen highlights the Celtics' 108-81 victory over the Milwaukee Bucks, emphasizing contributions from Derrick White and Hugo Gonzalez.
Despite being a Knicks fan, Eisen jokes about his children being Boston fans, attributing it to seeking a better life.

title: Boston 25 News reporter Drew Karedes leaves after nine years
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Drew Karedes announced his departure from Boston 25 News after nine years as a general assignment reporter.
Karedes, a North Shore native, began his journalism career 19 years ago in Green Bay, Wisconsin.
During his tenure, Karedes covered local stories including crime, traffic incidents, and an ICE arrest in Lynn.

title: 12 vehicles involved in series of crashes on I-95 ramp in Danvers
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Twelve vehicles were involved in crashes on an I-95 ramp in Danvers due to icy conditions.
Three separate crashes occurred on the I-95 North to Route 1 North ramp at Exit 70.
Three adults sustained back injuries; all involved were taken to local hospitals for evaluation.

title: 2 people, including child, hit by car in Dorchester
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An adult and a child were hit by a car in Dorchester Tuesday night.
Both victims were transported to a local hospital with minor injuries, according to police.
The incident occurred at the intersection of Mora Street and Washington Street; the driver remained at the scene.

title: Auburn police seek missing Mass. woman last seen in Florida
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jennifer Remillard, 47, was last seen on January 31 in Sarasota, Florida, according to Auburn Police.
Remillard, from Leicester Street in Auburn, is described as 5'9", 130 lbs, with blonde hair.
She may be traveling cross-country in a gray 2012 Toyota Camry with Massachusetts plate RS11JR.

title: Boston’s innovation economy hits a new milestone in global rankings
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Five Boston-area firms made it to the LexisNexis 'Top 100 Global Innovators' list, a record for the region.
Boston tied with L.A. for third in U.S. on the list, after Silicon Valley and New York City.
In global rankings, Boston would place sixth if it were a country, following the U.S., Germany, China, Korea, and Japan.

title: Is Scituate native Conor Garland a worthwhile trade target for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Conor Garland, a right wing for Vancouver Canucks, is signed through 2031-32 with a $6 million cap hit.
The Boston Bruins are considering Garland for his offensive skills but hesitate due to his long-term $36 million contract.
Trading for Garland is weighed against the team's rebuilding timeline and potential draft and prospect costs.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 4)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A Boston condo at 100 Belvidere St. sold for $3.7 million, the most expensive in the area this week.
A three-family building at 52 Snow Hill St., Boston, sold for $1.25 million, featuring 8 rooms and 3 bedrooms.
An Arlington ranch on 203 Everett St., built in 1956, was sold for $1.975 million with 1,409 sq ft.

title: Patriots might have shot at top free-agent receiver after Colts’ franchise-tag gamble
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Alec Pierce may become a free agent after the Colts opted not to use the franchise tag on him.
Pierce led the NFL in yards per catch for two consecutive seasons, making him a valuable target for the Patriots.
The Patriots would need to manage cap space to afford Pierce, potentially restructuring Stefon Diggs' contract.

title: Vermont postal worker allegedly threw away mail she was supposed to deliver for months
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Natasha Morisseau, 34, of North Troy, was cited for allegedly discarding mail intended for delivery.
Police found opened and unopened packages and holiday cards in a dumpster and Morisseau's mail vehicle.
Since Jan. 23, Morisseau has been suspended by USPS, and the mail was returned for delivery.
